gaps: No existing test case covers Fit Test functionality at all — all 15 test cases are about onboarding/EULA/location permission flows.
Missing test: Fit Test behavior when earbud buttons are pressed during the test — specifically the primary vs. non-primary earbud button press distinction.
Missing test: Fit Test error handling for 'device disconnected' message on primary earbud button press.
Missing test: Fit Test error handling for 'cannot complete test' message on non-primary earbud button press.
Missing test: Fit Test state management — what happens to the fit test audio/state when a hardware button interrupt occurs mid-test.
Missing test: Fit Test restart flow — verifying the test can be cleanly restarted after a failed/interrupted attempt.
Missing test: Device-specific asymmetry — the bug mentions Emu, Tern, or Osprey earbuds, but no test validates Fit Test behavior across different earbud models.
Missing test: Fit Test lifecycle handling — what happens if the app is backgrounded or a call comes in during the fit test audio playback.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Primary Earbud Button Press During Test Shows 'Device Disconnected' Error
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, press the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud (left/right as designated by the device).
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays the error message 'Device disconnected' on the Fit Test screen. The fit test audio stops. The test is marked as failed/interrupted.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Non-Primary Earbud Button Press During Test Shows 'Cannot Complete Test' Error
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, press the hardware button on the NON-PRIMARY earbud (the opposite earbud from the primary).
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays the error message 'Cannot complete test' on the Fit Test screen. The fit test audio stops. The test is marked as failed/interrupted.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Clean Restart After Primary Earbud Button Interrupt ('Device Disconnected')
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Press the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud to trigger the 'Device disconnected' error.
Verify the error message is displayed and the test is stopped.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' again to restart the fit test.
Allow the fit test to complete without interruption.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test restarts cleanly from the beginning. The fit test audio plays normally. The test completes successfully with a fit test result displayed (e.g., 'Good fit' or 'Adjust earbuds'). No residual error state from the previous failed attempt.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Clean Restart After Non-Primary Earbud Button Interrupt ('Cannot Complete Test')
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Press the hardware button on the NON-PRIMARY earbud to trigger the 'Cannot complete test' error.
Verify the error message is displayed and the test is stopped.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' again to restart the fit test.
Allow the fit test to complete without interruption.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test restarts cleanly from the beginning. The fit test audio plays normally. The test completes successfully with a fit test result displayed (e.g., 'Good fit' or 'Adjust earbuds'). No residual error state from the previous failed attempt.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Sequential Button Interrupts (Primary Then Non-Primary) During Test
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Press the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud — verify 'Device disconnected' error appears.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to restart.
While fit test audio is playing again, press the hardware button on the NON-PRIMARY earbud — verify 'Cannot complete test' error appears.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to restart again.
Allow the fit test to complete without interruption.
generated_testcases.expected: Each button press produces the correct error message for the respective earbud. The test can be cleanly restarted after each interrupt. The final test completes successfully.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — App Backgrounded During Fit Test Audio Playback (Lifecycle Handling)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list.
Observe the Fit Test screen state and audio state.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test audio stops when the app is backgrounded. Upon returning to the app, the Fit Test screen shows the test as interrupted/failed. The user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin a new test.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Incoming Call During Fit Test Audio Playback (Lifecycle Handling)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, trigger an incoming call on the connected phone (use a second device to call the test phone).
Observe the Fit Test screen state and audio behavior.
Reject or end the call.
Return to the Poly Studio Mobile app and observe the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test audio is interrupted by the incoming call. The Fit Test screen shows the test as failed/interrupted. After the call ends and the app is reopened, the user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin a new test.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — App Force-Killed During Fit Test Audio Playback (Lifecycle Handling)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps or use OS settings).
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Observe the Fit Test screen state.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test audio stops when the app is killed. Upon reopening the app and navigating to Fit Test, the screen shows the default state (no active test). The user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin a new test. No crash or stuck state occurs.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Device-Specific: Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC (Emu) Primary Earbud Button Press
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, press the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud (as designated by the device).
Observe the on-screen message.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays the error message 'Device disconnected' on the Fit Test screen. The fit test audio stops. This confirms the Emu device exhibits the primary earbud bug behavior.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Normal Completion After Successful Fit Test (No Button Interrupt)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Do NOT press any earbud buttons during the test.
Allow the fit test audio to play to completion.
Observe the fit test result.
generated_testcases.expected: The fit test completes successfully. A result message is displayed (e.g., 'Good fit' or 'Adjust earbuds for better fit'). No error messages appear. The test is marked as completed.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Rapid Consecutive Button Presses on Primary Earbud During Test
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, rapidly press the PRIMARY earbud button 5 times in quick succession.
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays the error message 'Device disconnected' on the Fit Test screen. The fit test audio stops. The app does not crash or enter an unstable state. The user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to restart.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Rapid Consecutive Button Presses on Non-Primary Earbud During Test

While fit test audio is playing, rapidly press the NON-PRIMARY earbud button 5 times in quick succession.
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays the error message 'Cannot complete test' on the Fit Test screen. The fit test audio stops. The app does not crash or enter an unstable state. The user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to restart.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Simultaneous Both Earbud Button Press During Test
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, press the hardware buttons on BOTH earbuds simultaneously.
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays an error message (either 'Device disconnected' or 'Cannot complete test' — whichever is triggered first). The fit test audio stops. The app does not crash or show conflicting/duplicate error messages.

generated_testcases.title: Fit Test — Bluetooth Disconnect During Fit Test Audio Playback
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While fit test audio is playing, disconnect the earbuds via phone Bluetooth settings or by powering off the earbuds.
Observe the on-screen message and state of the Fit Test screen.
Reconnect the earbuds.
Navigate back to Fit Test and attempt to start a new test.
generated_testcases.expected: The app displays a 'Device disconnected' or connection lost error message. The fit test audio stops. After reconnecting, the user can tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin a new test cleanly.
